followups.original_subject: Harewood Accountants — website not showing on mobile searches
followups.subject: One more thing about Harewood Accountants' Google visibility
followups.body:
David,

Hope the break was good — dropping a quick line to follow up on my note from last week.

I had a look at how Harewood Accountants appears in local Google searches. The mobile issue is part of it, but there's also a gap in your Google Business Profile that's holding back your ranking for 'accountant Wetherby' searches. A couple of small changes there could move you up significantly.

Happy to run a free local visibility check and share what I find — usually takes about 10 minutes.

[Your name]
followups.notes: SEND THIS MORNING. OOO expired yesterday (Monday). David had one day to settle back in. Warm opener ('hope the break was good') is appropriate and genuine. New angle: Google Business Profile gap rather than re-pitching the mobile issue. Professional services — keep tone businesslike but personal. Check IMAP first — if David has already replied since returning, do NOT send.

followups.subject: One more thing I spotted for Halifax Window Cleaning
followups.body:
Steve,

Following up on my note from last week about the SSL issue.

I had another look — halifaxwindowcleaning.co.uk also isn't appearing in Google Maps for 'window cleaner Halifax' searches despite having a listing. The missing SSL is part of the reason — Google ranks secure sites higher in local results — but there are also a couple of Business Profile fields that aren't filled in, which is easy to sort.

Happy to run a free check on both and send you what I find.

[Your name]
followups.notes: DUE 2026-04-09. Pre-written. New angle: local SEO ranking and Google Maps visibility rather than just SSL fix.

followups.original_subject: Pontefract Pharmacy — website not showing on mobile searches
followups.subject: Something else I noticed about Pontefract Pharmacy online
followups.body:
Hi,

Following up from last week's note about the mobile issue on the pharmacy site.

I had a look at your Google presence and noticed Pontefract Pharmacy's listing is missing a few things — no opening hours for Bank Holidays, no category for 'NHS pharmacy', and no response to any of your Google reviews. These are the signals Google uses to decide which pharmacies to show first in local search.

Small changes, significant difference. Happy to share a free report on what's missing.

[Your name]
followups.notes: DUE 2026-04-09. Bank Holiday hours topical — Easter is 18-21 April. Generic inbox — new angle must be very concrete.

followups.original_subject: Ann — Chrome flags your Castleford carpet site as unsafe
followups.subject: One thing I spotted for Castleford Carpets — Ann
followups.body:
Ann,

Following up from last week on the SSL issue.

I had another look at castlefordcarpets.co.uk and noticed there's no way for someone to request a quote or home visit online — the site shows your products, but there's no call-to-action to convert a browser into an enquiry.

For a flooring business, most customers want to visit in person but they start online. A simple 'Request a free quote' button would capture those leads before they find someone else.

Happy to show you what this looks like in practice — quick call if that helps?

[Your name]
followups.notes: DUE 2026-04-09. Shift from technical (SSL) to commercial (conversion gap). Flooring businesses respond better to revenue arguments than technical fixes.
